results.change_action: must be compared as part of an epoch/sequence number pair; data with a pair after that of the valid received closure alert must be ignored
results.change_condition: Any data is received after a valid received closure alert
results.related_state_or_step: processing received data after closure alert
results.comment: wolfSSL processing close_notify [non-English text removed] epoch/sequence pair [non-English text removed]。
results.comparison_summary: RFC 9147 [non-English text removed] epoch/sequence pair [non-English text removed]。
results.category: close_notify [non-English text removed]missing epoch/sequence pair [non-English text removed]
results.standard_check: RFC 9147 Section 5.10 requires implementations to remember the epoch/sequence number pair of a valid received closure alert and ignore later data whose pair is after that alert.
results.code_check: DoAlert records alert_history and sets closeNotify, and ProcessReply returns ZERO_RETURN on close_notify, but no source path stores close_notify curEpoch64/curSeq or compares later records against that saved pair.
results.test_check: verify_wolfssl_dtls13_151_187.ps1 confirms positive DTLS 1.3 record/ACK paths and the current build flags. A packet-level post-close pair test is blocked because the local CMake build has WOLFSSL_DTLS13=no and WOLFSSL_DTLS_CID=no, but static symbol/path review confirms the missing pair gate.
results.decision_reason: Generic closure handling exists, but the DTLS 1.3 pair-based ignore requirement is not implemented or exposed in the inspected paths.

results.comment: wolfSSL [non-English text removed] RFC 9147 NewConnectionId/RequestConnectionId [non-English text removed] usage=cid_spare/cid_immediate semantic。
results.comparison_summary: RFC 9147 [non-English text removed] NewConnectionId/RequestConnectionId [non-English text removed] cid_spare、cid_immediate [non-English text removed] wolfSSL_dtls_cid_* API、TLSX_ConnectionID_* [non-English text removed] NewConnectionId、RequestConnectionId、cid_spare、cid_immediate [non-English text removed]。
results.standard_check: RFC 9147 Section 9 defines NewConnectionId and RequestConnectionId messages and the usage values cid_spare and cid_immediate for dynamic CID rotation.
results.code_check: wolfSSL exposes RFC 9146-style Connection ID APIs and unified-header CID parsing, but repository-wide scans find no NewConnectionId, RequestConnectionId, cid_spare, or cid_immediate implementation.
results.test_check: verify_wolfssl_dtls13_151_187.ps1 scanned source paths and logged ABSENT for NewConnectionId, RequestConnectionId, cid_spare, and cid_immediate. The local build has WOLFSSL_DTLS13=no and WOLFSSL_DTLS_CID=no, so no executable dynamic-CID packet test is available.
results.decision_reason: The required protocol messages and usage semantics are absent; existing CID support only covers negotiated CID extension/header processing.
